title: Minuartia stojanovii
taxonomy: Minuartia stojanovii is a species of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. It is a member of the genus Minuartia, commonly known as pearlwort, which includes around 120 species. Minuartia stojanovii is native to the Balkan Peninsula and has been recorded in several countries, including Bulgaria, Greece, and North Macedonia. The plant is typically found in mountainous regions, growing in rocky areas and meadows. Minuartia stojanovii is a relatively rare species, and its classification within the broader Caryophyllaceae family is consistent with its botanical characteristics.

intro: Minuartia stojanovii, also known as Stojanov's sandwort, is a species of flowering plant in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae) that is native to the Balkan Peninsula. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including gastrointestinal problems and respiratory infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Minuartia stojanovii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Minuartia stojanovii include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat digestive issues, coughs, and colds.
